node.title: Uber | Onsite-DSA | Customer creation and query

topic.title: Uber | Onsite-DSA | Customer creation and query

post.content:
Implement the following functions:
1.addCustomer(double revenue) → Adds a new customer with a unique ID and assigns the specified revenue, representing the amount the customer brings to the company.
2. addCustomer(double revenue, string referredBy) → Adds a new customer with the given revenue and records the referring customer. The revenue of the referring customer is also increased by the revenue of the newly added customer.

	Example:
	1 (100) → 2 (50) → 3 (30)
	Customer 2 is referred by customer 1.
	Customer 3 is referred by customer 2.
	Final revenue distribution:

	Customer 1: 150 (100 + 50)
	Customer 2: 80 (50 + 30)
	Customer 3: 30
3. getKCustomersWithRevenueGreaterThanValue(int k, double value) → Returns the IDs of the first k customers whose revenue is greater than the given value.

	Example:
	Customer ID (Revenue)
	1 (100)
	2 (120)
	3 (150)
	4 (200)
	5 (300)
	If k = 2 and value = 90, the function should return customers 1 and 2.

`Follow up - For getTopKCustomerWithGreaterValue, the expected solution should have a time complexity of less than O(N log K). Since the dataset can be large or in stream and this query is frequent, constructing a priority queue for each query is not feasible.`

Maintain two maps , one hashmap with key as customerID and revenue as value . Another TreeMap with Revenue as key and Set of customerID as value.

Lets call these map and treemap respectively . Every time a new customer comes in , if there is no referrer add it to both map and treemap. If there is a referrer , this mean that we need to update the revenue of the referrer. to do that ,follow below steps

-> oldRevenue of referrer = map.get(referrerID);

-> newRevenue of referrer = oldRevenue of referrer + referralValue

-> update this newRevenue value to map

-> remove referrerID from set associated with oldRevenue , if this set is empty now , remove this key

-> add this newRevenue value to a set associated with newRevenue if exists otherwise create a new set and add id.



to fetch topK , just do treemap.tailMap()
For the top K part, I think they expected you to use the quickselect algorithm with median of medians, so the top k algorithm will run in a O(n) even in a worst case.\n\n
Hi OP, \nthis question was asked in `Depth in specialization` round?\nand for which role?
